qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Multipart form-data parser missing total size and field count limits (DoS)
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: No match: this finding concerns missing formidable multipart parser resource limits on webhook endpoints, while the advisory concerns Python Code Node Task Runner sandbox escape to arbitrary code execution.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Prototype Pollution via Object.assign with User-Controlled JSON
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: No match: this finding concerns JavaScript node parameter handling through Object.assign and JSON.parse, while the advisory concerns Python Task Runner sandbox escape from Python Code Node execution.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described Python Task Runner sandbox escape, Python Code Node boundary failure, or equivalent source-to-sink path for arbitrary code execution in the task runner container. The final qualifying findings are separate issues: webhook multipart parser resource exhaustion and JavaScript node Object.assign/JSON.parse handling.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-44v6-jhgm-p3m4 describes an authenticated Python Code Node user escaping the Python Task Runner sandbox to achieve arbitrary code execution in the task runner container.
.judge/input_manifest.json lists the final qualifying findings as multipart-dos and prototype-pollution-object-assign.
multipart-dos/vulnerability_report.json identifies missing formidable multipart parser limits in packages/cli/src/webhooks/webhook-form-data.ts, not Python task-runner sandbox code.
prototype-pollution-object-assign/vulnerability_report.json identifies Object.assign(JSON.parse(...)) patterns in JavaScript nodes, not Python Code Node sandbox escape logic.
